skill.name: academic-abstract-refiner
skill.description: Refines long medical academic texts into SCI-style unstructured Chinese and English abstracts; use when you need to condense drafts, reports, or summaries into bilingual abstracts and generate Summary_Report.md.
skill.summary: Transform lengthy medical research drafts into publication-ready bilingual abstracts in SCI journal format.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 29 potential issues including weak cryptographic algorithms, system reconnaissance, file operations, shell execution patterns, and hardcoded URLs. After manual evaluation, 27 of 29 findings were confirmed as false positives: the static analyzer misidentified documentation text, markdown code blocks, and filenames as security patterns. The two genuine findings (file write at refine_abstract.py:34 and os.makedirs at line 33) represent the skill's intended functionality with minimal risk. This is a straightforward text formatting skill with no network access, no credential handling, no code injection vectors, and no prompt injection attempts.

low_findings.title: File System Write via User-Specified Path
low_findings.description: The refine_abstract.py script writes output to a file path specified by the --output CLI argument. This is the documented purpose of the skill (generating Summary_Report.md). The written content is entirely user-provided abstract text with no sensitive data. No exfiltration risk exists since the script makes no network calls.

low_findings.title: Directory Creation via os.makedirs
low_findings.description: The script creates parent directories as needed for the output path. This is standard Python practice for ensuring writable output destinations. The path derives from user-provided CLI input but carries no injection risk in this context.

content.user_title: Refine Medical Texts into SCI-Style Bilingual Abstracts
content.value_statement: Long medical research drafts are difficult to condense into publication-ready abstracts. This skill transforms lengthy academic texts into concise, SCI-style bilingual abstracts in Chinese and English, saving hours of manual formatting and ensuring consistent output.
actual_capabilities: Converts long medical and academic texts into unstructured single-paragraph abstracts
Generates bilingual abstracts in both Chinese and English within one report
Produces a standardized Markdown deliverable as Summary_Report.md
Enforces formal academic tone consistent with SCI journal conventions
Maintains strict source fidelity without fabricating data or conclusions
Operates entirely offline with no external API calls or credentials required
limitations: Cannot generate abstracts without user-provided source text to work from
Does not perform literature searches, data extraction, or citation management
Output quality depends directly on the completeness and clarity of input text
Limited to the unstructured single-paragraph abstract format without section headers

use_cases.title: Condense Clinical Study Reports
use_cases.description: Transform lengthy clinical trial reports into concise bilingual abstracts suitable for journal submission or internal review, maintaining all key findings without adding unsupported claims.

use_cases.title: Prepare Thesis Abstracts for Publication
use_cases.description: Convert thesis chapters or dissertation sections into SCI-style unstructured abstracts in Chinese and English for conference proceedings or journal submission.

use_cases.title: Standardize Team Abstract Output
use_cases.description: Apply consistent SCI-style formatting across multiple research papers from a team, ensuring all abstracts follow the same bilingual unstructured format.
use_cases.target_user: Research team leads and lab managers

prompt_templates.title: Basic Abstract Request
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me condense this medical text into a bilingual abstract in Chinese and English using the academic-abstract-refiner workflow.
prompt_templates.scenario: User has a raw medical text and wants a first-pass bilingual abstract.

prompt_templates.title: Clinical Report Summarization
prompt_templates.prompt: I have a clinical study report about [topic]. Please generate an unstructured SCI-style abstract in both Chinese and English. Keep the tone formal and academic. Do not add any findings not present in the source text. Output the result using the refine_abstract.py script to Summary_Report.md.
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs a polished bilingual abstract from a structured clinical report.

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Source Synthesis
prompt_templates.prompt: Take the following research notes and evidence synthesis covering [topics]. Produce a single-paragraph unstructured abstract in Chinese and English. Ensure all claims are traceable to the provided sources and format the output as Summary_Report.md using the refine_abstract.py script with --abstract-zh and --abstract-en arguments.
prompt_templates.scenario: User has compiled notes from multiple papers and needs a unified abstract.

prompt_templates.title: Full Pipeline Execution
prompt_templates.prompt: Process this manuscript through the complete academic-abstract-refiner pipeline: (1) Extract the core findings and methodology from the text, (2) Draft an unstructured Chinese abstract in formal SCI style, (3) Draft the equivalent English abstract, (4) Verify both abstracts stay faithful to the source, (5) Render the output to Summary_Report.md using python scripts/refine_abstract.py --abstract-zh "..." --abstract-en "..." --output Summary_Report.md.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants the complete end-to-end workflow with validation steps.

output_examples.input: A 5000-word medical review article on recent advances in cardiovascular disease treatment covering drug therapies, surgical interventions, and lifestyle modifications
output_examples.output: A Summary_Report.md file containing a Chinese abstract and an English abstract. Each abstract is a single unstructured paragraph capturing the review's scope, key therapeutic developments, and main conclusions without fabricating statistics or adding opinions beyond the source text.

output_examples.input: Clinical trial results for a novel diabetes medication including patient demographics, methodology, efficacy data, and adverse event analysis
output_examples.output: A bilingual abstract report with formal SCI-style wording. Both abstracts present the study objective, approach, key efficacy findings, and safety profile in a single continuous paragraph each, with all numerical results and conclusions directly traceable to the original trial data.

best_practices: Provide the complete source text rather than excerpts to ensure the abstract captures all key findings and context accurately.
Verify that the Chinese and English abstracts convey equivalent meaning and include the same key information before finalizing the report.
Run python scripts/refine_abstract.py --help first to confirm the Python environment and script path are correctly configured before full execution.
anti_patterns: Do not ask the skill to generate an abstract without providing any source material or with only a vague topic description.
Do not expect the skill to perform literature reviews, find references, or add insights beyond what is present in the provided text.
Avoid requesting structured abstracts with section headers such as Objective, Methods, Results, and Conclusion.

faq.question: What types of documents can this skill process?
faq.answer: The skill accepts long medical and academic texts including review articles, clinical study reports, research summaries, and thesis chapters provided as plain text or pasted content.

faq.question: Does this skill call external AI models or APIs?
faq.answer: No. The Python script is a local formatter that writes abstract text into a Markdown report. It does not call any LLM, external API, or require internet access.

faq.question: What output format does the skill produce?
faq.answer: It produces a Summary_Report.md file containing both the Chinese and English abstracts as separate sections in a single Markdown document.

faq.question: Can I use this skill for non-medical academic texts?
faq.answer: Yes. The abstract refinement workflow works for any academic discipline that follows SCI-style conventions, though it was originally designed for medical research writing.

faq.question: Does the skill require an API key or special credentials?
faq.answer: No. The rendering script requires only Python 3.8 or later. It does not need API keys, authentication tokens, or any external service credentials.

faq.question: How do I verify the skill is set up correctly before use?
faq.answer: Run python scripts/refine_abstract.py --help in your terminal. If the command displays usage information, the script path and Python environment are properly configured.
